content:
Hi!

My name is Jordan Uggla and I'm interested in contributing to rust for linux.

I'm generally interested in filesystems. From my most recent job I have experience troubleshooting issues with Nvidia GPUs in RDMA clusters, including SRIOV (with Mellanox cards, passed into a qemu/kvm guest) with both infiniband and RoCE.

I have done a decent amount of C development for 8 bit atmel microcontrollers, and I went through the rust book and rustlings in 2021 but haven't done much rust development since then. I'm going through rustlings again to get back to proficiency.

I'm not sure where to start for contributing (or getting my skills up to where they're needed to meaningfully contribute).

Last I checked https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/contribute and https://docs.kernel.org/gpu/nova/core/todo.html there seemed to already be patches submitted by others for all of the issues.

I have a GeForce RTX 3080 Mobile in my laptop, so theoretically I could work on / test Nova stuff, but from listening to Dave Airlie from https://se-radio.net/2026/06/se-radio-723-dave-airlie-on-linux-kernel-maintenance/ , that doesn't seem like a good entry point at the moment.

I'm honestly wondering if there's a relatively cheap piece of unsupported hardware that I could buy just to have something to develop a driver for?

Anyway, thank you for reading this rambling introduction and apologies if this was the wrong place for something like this.

I was about to create a good first issue that happened today -- you could take that one maybe. It may be too simple, though, but if you want to learn the patch workflow etc. if you are new to the kernel, then it may be a good choice.

Welcome!
I would love a simple bug that I can use to learn the patch workflow. Thanks!
